english: rma-_jig (rma 'jig) Med. was explained: healing wounds. _jig-par byed-pa ('jig par byed pa) = _jig-pa ('jig pa), frq. — 2. vb. n. pf. bźig (bzhig), and more frq. źig (zhig), W. *źig-ce (zhig ce), źig čá-ce (zhig ca ce)*, to be ruined, undone, e.g. by mischief-making people Dzl.; to fall to pieces, to decay, to rot, of the human body etc.; to be lost, to perish, _jig-par śiṅ-tu sla ('jig par shing tu sla) (earthly goods) may be easily lost again Thgy.; to vanish, disappear, _jig ('jig) (or źig (zhig))-par gyur-ba ('gyur ba) id.; sem (sems) źig (zhig) soṅ (song) W. he was quite dejected or cast down; źig gsós byed-pa (zhig gsos byed pa) B., C., *źig-só (zhig gso) (or -sób (-sob)) čó-ce (co ce) or táṅ-ce (tang ce)* W. to 'restore from destruction', to rebuild c. dat. frq., also c. genit. Pth.; prob. also c. accus. — 3. to suck, draw out moisture Sch., v. _jib-pa ('jib pa). II. sbst. decay, destruction, ruin, entire overthrow, skyé-ba (skye ba) daṅ (dang) _jig-pa ('jig pa) kún-la (kun la) srid-na (srid na) as it is the lot of all men to rise and to decay Dzl.; lús-kyi (lus kyi) mtar (mthar) _jig-pai ('jig pa'i) ltas (ltas) symptoms of the final decay of the body Wdn.; *čáṅ-la (chang la) ḱoi (kho'i) źig-pa (zhig pa) yod (yod)* beer proves his ruin, beer is his destruction W.; _jig-pe ('jig pe) čó-čen (co can)* C., _jig-pa-čan ('jig pa can) Cs. frail, perishable. III. adj., but only in conjunction with a negative: mi-_jig-pa (mi 'jig pa) imperishable; mi-_jig (mi 'jig) rtág-pa (rtag pa) as explanation of a synonym Lex.

I. vb. (भी) resp. tsábs-pa (tshabs pa), to be afraid of a thing, is gen. connected with the instr. (lit. 'by'), in later literature and col. with la (la), srin-pos (srin pos) _jigs-śiṅ ('jigs shing) from fear of the Rakshasa Dzl.; dé-la (de la) ṅa (nga) mi (mi) _jigs ('jigs) I am not afraid of that Mil.; in W. frq. in conjunction with *rag (rag)*: *ḱó-la (kho la) _jig ('jig) rag (rag)* I am afraid of him; also relative to the future, like dógs-pa (dogs pa): yi-ge (yi ge) máṅs-pas (mangs pas) _jigs-nas ('jigs nas) = máṅ-gi (mang gi) dógs-nas (dogs nas), fearing lest there should be too much writing, i.e. from want of room Pth.; _jigs-su-ruṅ-ba ('jigs su rung ba) dreadful, frightful, frq.; *_jig-te ('jig te) dár-ri (dar ri) spé-ra (spe ra) zér-ce (zer ce)* W. to speak trembling and shaking with fear; *máṅ-po (mang po) _jig ('jig) soṅ (song)* W. I am very much afraid; _jigs-par ('jigs par) gyur-ba ('gyur ba) to be frightened, _jigs-par ('jigs par) byéd-pa (byed pa) to put in fear, to frighten. II. sbst. (भीति) fear, dread, sriṅ-poi (srin po'i) _jigs-pas ('jigs pas) from fear of the Rakshasa Dzl. MNg. 14 (unless sriṅ-pos (srin pos) ought to be read, as above); _jigs-pa ('jigs pa) brgyad (brgyad) the eight fears of life (so among the rest: rgyál-poi (rgyal po'i) _jigs-pa ('jigs pa) the standing in fear of the king, who in the East is always supposed to be an arbitrary despot); mi-_jigs-pa (mi 'jigs pa) 1. fearlessness, intrepidity; mi-_jigs-pa (mi 'jigs pa) sbyiṅ-pa (sbyin pa) to impart intrepidity; mi-_jigs-pai (mi 'jigs pa'i) lág-pa (lag pa) a fearless hand, heroic vigour. 2. pardon, quarter, safety Cs. — _jigs (-pa)-čan ('jigs pa can) Cs. 1. fearful, timorous. 2. dreadful, frightful (I never found it used in this sense). III. adj. 1. (fearing) fearful, timorous, _jigs-pai ('jigs pa'i) gró-ba-rnams ('gro ba rnams) timorous beings Pth. — 2. (feared) dreadful, frightful, _jigs-pai ('jigs pa'i) mtsón-ča (mtshon cha) dreadful weapons; ḱyod-pas (khyod pas) lhag-par (lhag par) _jigs-pa ('jigs pa) yod (yod) there is something even more formidable than you are Dzl. Comp. bár-do-la (bar do la) _jigs ('jigs) skyób-mai (skyob ma'i) smón-lam (smon lam) a prayer efficacious in the Bardo-horrors Thgr. — _jigs-skrág ('jigs skrag) fear; also a terrible object, _jigs-skrág-tu ('jigs skrag tu) soṅ (song) he has been changed into a fright, a monster Mil.; *_jig-tág ('jig tag) tóm-pa (tom pa)* C. (lit. btón-pa (bton pa)) to frighten, deter; intimidate, threaten; _jigs-skrág-pa ('jigs skrag pa) to fear, to be afraid Dzl. — _jigs-mḱan ('jigs mkhan) col. timid, timorous. — _jigs-čan ('jigs can) v. _jigs-pa-čan ('jigs pa can) above. — _jigs-čúm-pa ('jigs cum pa) v. čum-pa (cum pa). — _jigs(-pa)-po ('jigs pa po) one afraid Cs. (?) — _jigs-byéd ('jigs byed) one that is terrifying Sch., appellation of Yamāntaka, who is invoked, e.g. in drawing lots. — _jigs-brál ('jigs bral), _jigs-méd ('jigs med) fearless, intrepid, bold; also noun pers. — *_jigs-ri ('jigs ri)* W. fear, terror, *_jig-ri ('jig ri) tsór-ce (tshor ce)* to be afraid, *_jig-ri ('jig ri) kúl-ce (skul ce)* to frighten, to menace, to intimidate. — _jigs-sa ('jigs sa) Mil., _jigs-sa ('jigs sa) čé (che) it is a very dangerous quarter or region, in that place there is much occasion for being afraid.

1. acc. to Cs. = mčiṅ (mching), kloṅ (klong), e.g. rgyá-mtsoi (rgya mtsho'i); Sch.: mtso-_jiṅ (mtsho 'jing) the whole circumference of a lake; prob. more corr.: the middle, Lex.: lus-_jám (lus 'jam) _jág-po ('jag po) mtsỏ-_jiṅ (mtsho 'jing) _jug ('jug) the smooth-bodied Lu alights in the middle of the lake. — 2. srod-_jiṅ (srod 'jing) Lex.; or srod-byiṅ (srod bying) twilight.
also mjiṅ-pa (mjing pa), neck, resp. sku-_jiṅ (sku 'jing); *jiṅ-pa ('jing pa) gyúr-ce ('gyur ce)* W. to
